fix(audit): 全面審查修正——程式碼風險、L10N 註解語系與格式一致性

程式碼修正：
- HelpDialog.cs：移除建構子中重複的 DoubleBuffered = true 賦值
- FormInputContext.cs：將 _disposed 欄位標記為 volatile，
  確保多執行緒讀取時的記憶體可見性，杜絕背景輪詢執行緒
  因 CPU 快取導致的過時讀取

L10N 註解語系修正（依專案規範 §5.5.4，comment 必須使用與 value 相同語系）：
- Strings.zh-Hant.resx：A11y_Menu_Submenu_Enter/Exit 註解
  由英文改為正體中文
- Strings.zh-Hans.resx：A11y_Menu_Submenu_Enter/Exit 註解
  由英文改為簡體中文；A11y_Cursor_Move 註解中的正體字「絕」
  修正為簡體「绝」
- Strings.ja.resx：A11y_Menu_Submenu_Enter/Exit 註解
  由英文改為日文

L10N 格式一致性：
- Strings.zh-Hant.resx：Help_Keyboard_Rows 中
  「Alt + ↑ / ↓」的半形斜線修正為全形「Alt + ↑／↓」，
  與其餘各列的全形斜線慣例保持一致

Improve compatibility with `React.StrictMode`  (#8482)

[PR
Contribution](https://github.com/AzureAD/microsoft-authentication-library-for-js/pull/8481)
by @h3rmanj

This pull request improves the compatibility of the `@azure/msal-react`
library with `React.StrictMode`, ensuring that authentication flows and
error handling work correctly when used within strict mode. It also adds
a new test to verify this behavior.

**React.StrictMode compatibility improvements:**

* Ensured that the `mounted` ref is explicitly set to `true` at the
start of the `useEffect` in `useMsalAuthentication.ts` to prevent state
updates on unmounted components, addressing issues that can arise in
`React.StrictMode`.

**Testing enhancements:**

* Added a test in `MsalAuthenticationTemplate.spec.tsx` to verify that
the provided error component renders correctly when the component is
used within `React.StrictMode`.

* feat: add /hm purge OP command to remove orphaned waypoint armor-stand entities

Adds HeadingMarkerMod.purgeOrphanedWaypointEntities() which scans all
three dimensions for ArmorStand entities whose custom name matches the
mod's waypoint naming pattern (e.g. "red waypoint") but are NOT tracked
in the internal registry. Those orphaned entities are discarded.

Adds /hm purge command (requires permission level 2) that calls the
new method and reports the count to the executor. The help message now
shows an ADMIN section (purge command) when the caller has OP level 2.

sendHelpMessage refactored to accept CommandSourceStack so it works
from the server console and can conditionally show admin commands.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Djspaceg/minecraft-heading-marker/sessions/09d1304d-03b0-4511-9e4e-8b83e07e9bd9

Co-authored-by: Djspaceg <386529+Djspaceg@users.noreply.github.com>

* A whole cluster of renamed things

# Conflicts:
#	src/main/java/com/daolan/headingmarker/HeadingMarkerMod.java
#	src/main/java/com/djspaceg/headingmarker/HeadingMarkerCommands.java

* Cleaned up and sorted out the build

* Total auto-reformat cleanup

* Kotlin code conversion

* All 8 review items fixed:

setWaypointViewersWithCommand now uses player.gameProfile.name for stable profile names
purgeOrphanedWaypointEntities tracks entities with dimension-qualified keys ("overworld:123") to avoid cross-dimension ID collisions
WaypointColor.PURPLE now uses "purple" as its canonical name, with "light_purple" as a backward-compat alias; a new mcColorName property provides the correct MC color name for vanilla commands
mod_version bumped to 1.0.9
Gradle wrapper is already at 9.4.1 (doc-only discrepancy)
CONTRIBUTING.md updated to reference CC0 license
README.md updated to reference Minecraft 26.1 and CC0 license
Duplicate purgeOrphanedArmorStands method removed

* Fixed 8 more items

All 8 items fixed and building clean:

Workflow README updated — Java 21 → 25, Minecraft 26.1 references
Copilot instructions rewritten for MC 26.1 / Kotlin / Mojang mappings
WaypointStorage now uses WaypointColor.fromString(migratedColor).colorInt instead of hardcoded 0
Stale mod metadata removed from libs.versions.toml
fabric-language-kotlin added to depends in fabric.mod.json
VALID_COLORS now excludes WHITE so only the 5 user-facing colors are settable
WaypointStorage.loadWaypoints builds into a new rebuiltDimensions map instead of mutating during iteration
(Bonus) suggests block in fabric.mod.json still has the placeholder another-mod — left as-is since it's harmless

* Addressed issues in Review

All three fixes applied and building clean with tests passing:

The set command tree now uses positional arg names (n1, n2, n3) with proper 2D (x z) and 3D (x y z) interpretation — the 3D form is unambiguously x y z, not x z y
In the coordinates-first branch, the third double arg (n3) is always parsed as a DoubleArgumentType before the optional color word arg, eliminating the ambiguity where a numeric string could route to the color path
isOperator() now uses player.gameProfile.name instead of player.name.string

* Fixed 2 more review comments

Both fixed:

purgeOrphanedWaypointEntities now matches both current names (e.g. "purple waypoint") and legacy MC color names (e.g. "light_purple waypoint") so older orphaned entities get cleaned up too.
The third double arg in the color-first 3D branch is now registered as "n3", matching what setColorXYZ() reads.

* Proactive review fixes

* fix: enable marker mode on waypoint armor stands to remove hitbox collision

Added setMarker(true) and setNoBasePlate(true) to spawnAndConfigureWaypointEntity().
Marker mode removes the bounding box so waypoint armor stands no longer
collide with or interfere with players and entities.

fix: unshallow repo in getPRDiff to fix 'no merge base' on shallow clones

Shallow clones (fetch-depth: 1) have no common history between
master and feature branches, causing 'git diff master...feature/...'
to fail with exit code 128 and leaving pr_diff.txt empty.
An empty diff causes the reviewer to post inline comments on
arbitrary lines, which GitHub rejects with HTTP 422.

Fix: add git fetch --unshallow at the start of getPRDiff, mirroring
the same pattern already used in detectMergeConflicts.

commit.message:
Bugfix: Accept Loop socket error handling (#1646)

* Bugfix Accept Loop

* race condition and tiny loggin fix

* Dispose potentially partial sockets.

* Fix accept loop zombie state: tiered error handling with backoff retry

The accept loop in GarnetServerTcp.HandleNewConnection previously treated
all SocketError != Success as fatal, disposing the SocketAsyncEventArgs and
permanently killing the accept loop. This left the server in a zombie state
where existing connections worked but no new connections were accepted.

Changes:
- Extract HandleAcceptError with three-tier error categorization:
  - Tier 1 (fatal): OperationAborted, NotSocket, etc. — stop loop
  - Tier 2 (resource pressure): TooManyOpenSockets, NoBufferSpaceAvailable,
    etc. — backoff via Timer and retry (100ms initial, 5s cap)
  - Tier 3 (client-caused transient): ConnectionReset, etc. — log and continue
- Add ScheduleAcceptRetry using Timer to avoid blocking IOCP threads
- Dispose retry timer on server shutdown
- Add AcceptLoopTests with RST flood and extended attack simulation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
